name: Real-Time Detection of Hallucinated Entities in Long-Form Generation
authors: Oscar Obeso*, Andy Arditi*, Javier Ferrando, Joshua Freeman, Cameron Holmes, Neel Nanda (ETH Zürich, MATS — ML Alignment & Theory Scholars Program)
dataset_size: N/A — method paper; evaluated on LongFact and HealthBench (long-form), TriviaQA (short-form)
domain: N/A — method paper (long-form factual generation, health/medical, short-form QA, mathematical reasoning)
annotation_cost_per_sample: N/A — annotation done by Claude 4 Sonnet with web search; approximately seconds per entity span
signal_type: hidden-state (linear probes on intermediate residual stream layer)
white_box_requirements: Linear probes trained on hidden states from a specific intermediate layer: layer ell = floor(0.95 * num_layers). Optionally, LoRA adapters inserted into all layers preceding the head. Requires per-token hidden state access at inference time — this is white-box (internal representation access). Token-level labels: entity spans annotated as 'Supported', 'Not Supported', or 'Insufficient Information' by Claude 4 Sonnet + web search pipeline. Probe architecture: linear value head; optionally LoRA-augmented.
supervision: supervised (linear probe trained on labeled entity spans; nLFM = 8,000 long-form + nSFM = 2,000 short-form samples for primary models; 2,000 long-form for secondary models)
granularity: token-level (entity span level — each entity in the generated response gets a hallucination label)
temporal_resolution: streaming (real-time during generation; probe runs in same forward pass with negligible overhead)
integration_mode: post-hoc detector (probe trained offline, applied at inference time in the same forward pass) — also supports 'monitor and abstain' intervention: flag unsupported entities as tokens are produced
spectral_signature_overlap: none. Linear probes on residual-stream hidden states have no spectral, frequency-domain, or eigenvalue component. The method uses semantic entropy only as a baseline comparison (AUC 0.71 for Llama-3.3-70B), which they outperform (AUC 0.90). No FFT, no Laplacian eigenvalues, no Fourier analysis of entropy trajectories. Complete non-overlap with our spectral approach.
tested_at_what_length: LongFact (long-form generation tasks, typically hundreds to thousands of tokens); HealthBench (medical QA long-form); TriviaQA (short-form as comparison)
reported_value: Linear probe AUC: LongFact/Llama-3.1-8B: 0.854; LongFact/Llama-3.3-70B: 0.867; HealthBench/Llama-3.3-70B: 0.873; TriviaQA/Llama-3.3-70B: 0.948. LoRA probe AUC: LongFact/Llama-3.3-70B: 0.905; TriviaQA/Llama-3.3-70B: 0.983. Headline: AUC 0.90 (LoRA) vs 0.71 (semantic entropy) on Llama-3.3-70B for long-form generation.
overlapping_benchmark: TriviaQA (our Phase 9 CoT result: 53.6% Nadler; this paper's TriviaQA Llama-3.3-70B: 0.948/0.983 — dramatically better). Qwen-2.5-7B is also a shared model family.
applicability: high — long-form generation is exactly our Phase 10 target; streaming entity-level detection is complementary to our trajectory-level detection
preemption_risk: LOW-MEDIUM. This paper achieves excellent results (0.90 AUC) on long-form generation and clearly outperforms our spectral method on factual long-form tasks. However, it does NOT pre-empt our contribution because: (1) It is entity-level (token-level factual entity detection) while we are trajectory-level (does the full response reflect hallucination in its entropy dynamics). Different granularity and different failure modes addressed. (2) It requires ~8,000 labeled training samples — our Nadler fusion is unsupervised. (3) It uses hidden states (more access than our entropy-only approach). (4) It does not address reasoning-domain tasks (math, GPQA) where our spectral features shine — their benchmarks are factual long-form. (5) Our thesis claim (spectral features work on reasoning, not factual QA) is actually CONSISTENT with this paper's results — their probe works on factual QA precisely where our spectral approach fails. These are complementary, not competing. Main risk: if Phase 10 targets long-form factual RAG, this paper becomes a very strong baseline that we must beat or match.
bracha_alignment: High. The streaming probe flags entities with confidence scores — a natural target for Bracha's LTT/conformal calibration to provide finite-sample FDR/FNR guarantees on entity-level hallucination detection. Her COIN framework could also leverage the partially-labeled annotation pipeline (Claude 4 Sonnet web search labels) via semi-supervised calibration.
ofir_alignment: Low-Medium. Linear probes on residual stream are not spectral methods. However, Ofir's multi-view kernel framework could naturally combine this probe signal with our entropy-trajectory features as complementary views in a Nadler-style fusion — entity-level probes for factual long-form + spectral entropy for reasoning chains.
notes: This paper is from the mechanistic interpretability community (Neel Nanda's group / MATS). It represents best-in-class supervised entity hallucination detection for long-form generation. Our spectral method addresses different use cases (reasoning-heavy, math/science MCQ) where entity probes have not been evaluated. The two approaches are complementary: entity probes catch factual entity fabrication; spectral entropy features catch reasoning-domain failures. Public code and datasets at https://github.com/obalcells/hallucination_probes — worth examining for Phase 10 as an additional baseline. Annotation uses Claude 4 Sonnet with web search (84% human agreement, 80.6% recall on controlled hallucinations).
gpu_requirements: Single A100 sufficient for 7B–9B models; 70B models require multi-GPU or 80GB A100. Probe training uses CPU for linear probe, GPU for LoRA fine-tuning. Colab A100 (80GB) covers all except 70B with LoRA.
sample_size_for_replication: nLFM = 8,000 long-form + nSFM = 2,000 short-form samples for primary models; 2,000 long-form for secondary models
min_samples_for_replication: 2,000 samples (secondary model configuration) for a meaningful pilot
runtime_estimate: Probe inference runs in same forward pass with 'negligible computational overhead' per token. Training: full dataset annotation via Claude 4 Sonnet + web search pipeline (substantial cost); fine-tuning LoRA adapters requires additional GPU time per model.
cumulative_state_modeling: None — per-token/per-entity probe with no cumulative state tracking across the generation trajectory
